subject: Databases
title: Table Partitioning
description: A short lesson to help you apply Table Partitioning.

hook.text: A table has 2 billion rows. Queries only need this month's data. Partitioning lets the database skip the rest.

buildup.text: Partition by range: each partition holds one month. Queries on a date range scan only relevant partitions.

discovery.text: Partition pruning: the planner excludes partitions that can't match the WHERE clause. Huge I/O savings.

twist.text: Queries without a partition key in WHERE scan all partitions. Worse than no partitioning.

climax.text: Drop old partitions instead of deleting rows. DROP PARTITION is instant. DELETE is slow on billions of rows.

punchline.text: Partition big tables by time or key. Prune what you don't need.

quiz.question: Why is dropping a partition faster than deleting rows?
options: DROP PARTITION removes the entire data file instantly without scanning individual rows
Deleting rows requires an internet connection
Partitions are already empty by default
